reasoning:
The question asks if an AI will achieve >85% on the FrontierMath benchmark before Jan 1, 2026. 
FrontierMath is an incredibly difficult benchmark introduced by Epoch AI in Nov 2024. The initial SOTA was <2%. In Dec 2024, OpenAI claimed their o3 model achieved roughly 25%. However, recent independent testing in April 2025 by Epoch AI revealed that o3 actually only scores around 10%. 
To go from ~10% to >85% on such a difficult benchmark in roughly 8 months (before Jan 1, 2026) would require a monolithic leap in capabilities. While test-time scaling has shown promise, an Epoch AI researcher in Feb 2025 (predating the revelation that o3 was only ~10%) predicted the SOTA would hit a median of 75% by the end of 2025. Given that o3's true performance was vastly lower than believed at that point, the trajectory to >85% by end of 2025 seems wildly optimistic. Other models like Claude 3.7 have not shown performance significantly exceeding o3. I place the probability of reaching >85% by the end of 2025 at 6%.
